records.ety: [OF. atemprer, fr. L. attemperare; ad + temperare to soften, temper. See Temper, and cf. Attemperate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Attempered (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Attempering.]

defs: To reduce, modify, or moderate, by mixture; to temper; to regulate, as temperature.
quotes: If sweet with bitter . . . were not attempered still.

defs: To soften, mollify, or moderate; to soothe; to temper; as, to attemper rigid justice with clemency.
To mix in just proportion; to regulate; as, a mind well attempered with kindness and justice.
To accommodate; to make suitable; to adapt.
notes: ☞ This word is now not much used, the verb temper taking its place.
